title: User account locked repeatedly after a successful password reset

servicenow_seed.short_description: [TRAINING] Account locks again within minutes of every reset
servicenow_seed.description: Caller's account locks within 5 minutes of every password reset. Has happened 3 times today. Lockout source is unknown.

correct_steps: Unlock the account in AD and capture LastBadPasswordAttempt + lockout source DC
Ask whether the user has stored the old password on a phone, mapped drive, scheduled task, or another device
Check whether the user has multiple sessions on different devices
Escalate to IAM with the lockout source DC and any device names involved
rationale: A lockout that repeats after a clean reset means an unknown source is presenting the old credential. Tier 1 should not loop on resets - escalate to IAM with evidence.
hints: Capture the lockout source DC. That is the single most useful piece of evidence for IAM.
Check for stored credentials on phones and mapped drives.
learning_objectives: Recognize when a password reset will not solve the problem
Capture lockout source and device evidence for IAM

tool_clues.ad: Account: Locked. Bad password count: 5 in 4 minutes. Lockout source DC: DC-CORP-04.
tool_clues.intune: User has 2 enrolled devices. Last sign-in 2 minutes ago from LAP-1187.
